title: Internal incident review page
problemStatement: Incident reviews become hard to scan when impact, status, timeline, and follow-up actions compete for attention.
audience: Support leads, engineers, and product owners reviewing a service disruption
userGoal: Understand what happened, what is affected, and what action is needed next.
businessGoal: Create a shared record that improves response quality without assigning blame.

contextDetails.label: Decision needed
contextDetails.value: - [ ] Approve the hierarchy for executive readouts and team retros.

contextDetails.label: Out of scope
contextDetails.value: Root-cause taxonomy, alert configuration, and customer messaging templates.

contextDetails.label: Contributors
contextDetails.value: Support, platform engineering, product ops, communications

items.title: Incident status and customer impact
items.content: Resolved at 10:42 AM. Some file uploads failed for 18 minutes. No data loss detected.
annotations: Status, impact, and data-risk statement must stay above timeline details.

items.title: What changed and when
items.content: Deploy began at 10:13 AM, alerts fired at 10:21 AM, rollback started at 10:31 AM, service recovered at 10:42 AM.
annotations: Timeline can become a structured component in the editor.

items.title: Owner, next step, due date
items.content: Platform team will add upload smoke tests to release checks by Friday.
annotations: Action items need owner and due date before the guide is considered complete.
